data.title: Samsung Vs. SK Hynix: The High-Stakes Race To Perfect 'Hybrid Bonding' For Nvidia's Next AI Chips
data.description:
Hybrid Bonding Emerges As Key Battleground

The firm highlighted that rising demand for AI and high-performance computing is forcing the industry to move beyond current micro-bump technology, which is hitting limits in power, performance, and heat.

Ecosystem Shifts As Demand Pressures Rise

Counterpoint added that while relaxed standards and process sensitivity may slow adoption in the near term, the long-term shift to hybrid bonding remains inevitable.

Demand from Nvidia Corp’s (NASDAQ:NVDA) AI workloads is pushing suppliers to align their roadmaps and accelerate the transition as performance needs increase across the industry.

Supply Crunch And Competition Intensify

Meanwhile, AI-driven data center spending is tightening the global memory market, pushing prices higher and limiting supply for other sectors.

At the same time, Samsung and SK Hynix are stepping up competition in AI memory, with Samsung advancing HBM4 (High Bandwidth Memory 4) production while SK Hynix expands capacity and secures supply deals to defend its lead.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Why The S&P 500 Is Trading On Oil Prices, Not Earnings
data.description:
At 1:23 PM Eastern on April 2, ES futures hit 6,510, down nearly 100 points from their overnight high, before snapping back 130 points into the close on $2.8 billion in buy-side market-on-close orders. The contract settled at 6,624, essentially flat. The VIX barely moved. And yet, beneath the surface, the options market was screaming a single message: this isn’t over.

That mechanical close masked what institutional derivatives positioning was actually saying about the weeks ahead. With crude oil above $112, the Strait of Hormuz disrupted, Non-Farm Payrolls dropping into a three-day weekend with zero hedging ability, and dealer gamma deeply negative, the S&P 500 has entered a phase where oil prices matter more than anything coming out of earnings season. The data behind that thesis is worth examining closely.

The $112 Problem

The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively disrupted since the start of the Iran conflict. Iran prepared a monitoring pact with Oman, requiring shipping tolls. The USS Ford carrier repositioned to rejoin operations. A drone struck Iraq’s Trebil border crossing. None of this is de-escalation.

Crude at $112 isn’t contained to energy stocks. It feeds into consumer inflation expectations, manufacturing input costs, and transportation margins within weeks. Chicago Fed President Goolsbee called the oil price rise “quite serious” on April 2 and warned that extended increases would show up in inflation expectations, the scenario the Fed fears most because it forces them to hold rates steady while the economy weakens underneath.

Put simply, $112 oil is repricing the entire rate-cut timeline for the second half of 2026. Initial Jobless Claims came in at 202K versus 212K expected on the same day, showing the labor market still healthy enough that the Fed has no urgency to ease. Strong jobs plus rising oil equals the old stagflation calculus, where monetary policy has no good options.

What the Options Market Already Knows

When you strip away intraday noise, derivatives positioning tells a cleaner story than price action.

SPX put open interest sat at 11.84 million contracts versus 8.07 million calls on April 2. The 25-delta risk reversal printed at -0.085, confirming persistent put skew. Across the major index ETFs, net delta exposure was -$9.46 billion, ranking at the 87th percentile for bearish positioning. That’s not cautious. That’s directionally committed.

The largest premium trades reinforced this conviction. Microsoft puts dominated the session’s flow: $73 million in April 17 $460 puts, $72 million in $450 puts, $55 million in $475 puts. Five of the ten largest single-name option trades that day were MSFT downside protection. When institutions buy that much protection on a mega-cap name, they aren’t hedging one earnings report. They’re hedging a macro scenario that plays out over months.

One trade stood out above the rest. Someone bought 79,979 VIX 50-strike calls expiring October 2026, paying full premium. That trade isn’t a bet on a bad week. It’s a bet on sustained, elevated volatility through the back half of the year. The size and expiration suggest genuine institutional conviction that the current conditions have months to run, not days.

Dealer Gamma Is Amplifying Every Move

One of the less-discussed dynamics driving the market’s intraday behavior is how options dealer positioning has shifted.

Gamma notional was negative $236.86 million heading into the close. In practical terms, when large options dealers carry negative gamma, they hedge by selling into declines and buying into rallies, amplifying directional moves rather than dampening them. That explains why 100-point intraday swings on ES have become routine rather than exceptional.

A forward-looking stability reading was at just 16% at the close, meaning the conditions for a large move were coiled and present. Low readings don’t guarantee timing, but they tell you the structure is compressed and waiting for a catalyst.

Gap Risk Nobody Could Hedge

Non-Farm Payrolls released at 8:30 AM Friday, April 3. Markets were closed for Good Friday. Futures didn’t reopen until 6:00 PM Sunday. That’s 57.5 hours of exposure with no ability to adjust positions.

The week following April 7-10 was stacked: ISM Services on Monday, FOMC Minutes on Wednesday, Core PCE and GDP on Thursday, CPI on Friday. Four potential volatility events in five sessions, all hitting a market positioned at the 87th percentile for bearish delta with negative dealer gamma and a coiled stability reading.

Where the Pressure Points Are

The setup heading into Q2 isn’t ambiguous. Crude above $110 keeps inflation expectations elevated. The Fed can’t cut with oil-driven CPI running hot. Institutions are directionally committed to the downside. Dealer gamma amplifies moves in both directions. The stability reading is compressed.

Rallies into the ES 6,627-6,641 range, where dealer positioning flips and overhead gamma concentrates, are likely to attract selling. The path of least resistance stays lower until the Iran situation resolves or crude breaks back below $100.

The contrarian risk deserves mention because positioning is so one-sided. Any genuine de-escalation signal, a ceasefire announcement, diplomatic channels reopening, the strait normalizing, could trigger a violent unwind. The 80,000 VIX 50-strike calls and $300 million in MSFT puts tell you institutions see the asymmetry and are bracing for something worse, but they also know a peace headline could compress months of hedging into a single session.

The 200-day moving average on SPX sits at 6,644, hovering just above current price like a verdict that hasn’t been delivered yet.

Disclosure: The author may hold positions in ES futures, SPY options, and related instruments at the time of publication. Past results are not indicative of future performance. This content is for informational and educational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice or a recommendation to buy or sell any security or futures contract.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: What's Going On With Playtika Stock Monday?
data.description:
The board formed a special committee of independent directors to evaluate options across its portfolio and retained Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC as financial advisor.

Playtika said there is no assurance the review will result in a transaction and does not plan to provide updates unless the board approves a specific course of action.

The company added that the announcement does not constitute an offer to sell or solicit securities in any jurisdiction where such actions would be unlawful.

Seeking New Paths

Playtika develops and operates mobile games such as Board Kings, House of Fun, Poker Heat, Slotomania, and Bingo Blitz, supported by live game operations services and its proprietary technology platform. It primarily makes money by selling virtual items tied to its online games across both mobile and web-based platforms.

That business model can throw off steady cash flow when engagement is strong, but it can also face pressure when user acquisition costs rise or game portfolios mature. In that context, a strategic alternatives review is a way to explore options that could reshape the company's path—without guaranteeing any deal or outcome.

Playtika Q1 2026 Earnings

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 7, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: 12 cents (Up from 8 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $691.84 million (Down from $706.00 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold rating with an average price target of $7.70. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Goldman Sachs: Neutral (Lowers Target to $4.25) (March 2)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Lowers Target to $5.50) (February 20)
  • Freedom Capital Markets: Initiated with Hold (Target $3.75) (November 3, 2025)

PLTK Price Action: Playtika Holding shares were up 4.74% at $2.87 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo byThx4Stock team via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Red Cat Stock Monday?
data.description:
The company is expanding its Blue Ops division by collaborating with HADDY to implement advanced robotic production systems.

It is expected to effectively double its manufacturing capacity for Unmanned Surface Vessels (USVs).

Details

The partnership will allow Blue Ops to build a microfactory environment at its facility in Valdosta, Georgia, enhancing production efficiency and capacity.

This collaboration is part of a broader trend in maritime manufacturing, leveraging large-scale 3D printing to streamline the design and delivery of USVs.

Barry Hinckley, President of Blue Ops, said the development highlights a broader transformation in boat manufacturing. He noted that, much like the industry’s past shift from wood to fiberglass, large-scale 3D printing is now driving a similar evolution.

According to Hinckley, the technology significantly accelerates the transition from design to production while enabling manufacturers to scale output in ways not previously possible.

Recent UAS Order Wins

Last month, a NATO ally selected Red Cat’s Black Widow small unmanned aircraft system (sUAS) through a competitive tender, with delivery scheduled for this calendar year.

Also, Red Cat signed a strategic partnership with Ukraine’s Spetstechnoexport, a state-owned defense enterprise, to collaborate on next-generation unmanned and robotic systems.

Red Cat Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 13, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 13 cents (Improvement from Loss of 17 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $18.88 million (Increase from $2.78 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $17.67. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Needham: Buy (Raises Target to $20.00) (March 19)
  • Ladenburg Thalmann: Buy (Raises Target to $20.00) (March 3)
  • Needham: Buy (Maintains Target to $16.00) (March 2)

Red Cat Top ETF Exposure

  • State Street SPDR S&P Kensho Future Security ETF (NYSE:FITE): 2.16% Weight
  • REX Drone ETF (NASDAQ:DRNZ): 4.38% Weight

Significance: Because RCAT carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

RCAT Price Action: Red Cat Holdings shares were up 0.85% at $13.04 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.
